name: Apophis Extended
description: Apophis, also known as Apep, is the ancient Egyptian deity of chaos, darkness, and destruction, and the archenemy of the sun god Ra. He is a colossal serpent, born from the primordial darkness, existing before creation and seeking to return the world to its original state of non-existence. Apophis dwells in the Duat, the Egyptian underworld, and attempts to devour Ra each night as the sun god travels through it in his solar barque. The daily battle between Ra and Apophis is a crucial element of Egyptian cosmology, representing the constant struggle between order (Ma'at) and chaos (Isfet). Apophis is never fully defeated, embodying the enduring presence of chaos in the universe. His powers include immense strength, the ability to influence storms and natural disasters, and the capacity to hypnotize and corrupt other beings. He commands legions of demons and monstrous creatures that aid him in his nightly assaults. While Ra is protected by other deities like Set, Isis, and Thoth, Apophis' relentless attacks highlight the vulnerability of the cosmos and the importance of maintaining Ma'at. Temples and rituals were performed to ward off Apophis and ensure Ra's successful journey, securing the continuation of life and light. Apophis represents the ultimate threat to cosmic order, a constant reminder of the fragility of existence and the need for vigilance against the forces of darkness.
subtitle: Chaos Serpent, Enemy of Ra

keyMyths.title: The Daily Battle with Ra
keyMyths.description: Each night, Apophis attacks Ra's solar barque as it travels through the Duat. Ra, aided by other deities, fights off Apophis to ensure the sun rises again.

keyMyths.title: Apophis's Origin
keyMyths.description: Apophis is said to have been born from the primordial waters of Nun, representing the chaos that existed before creation and seeks to reclaim the world.

keyMyths.title: Warding off Apophis
keyMyths.description: Rituals and spells were performed by priests to protect Ra from Apophis, including burning effigies of the serpent and reciting incantations.

sources.author: E.A. Wallis Budge
sources.description: Provides insight into the beliefs surrounding the afterlife and the dangers faced within it, including Apophis.
sources.text: Provides insight into the beliefs surrounding the afterlife and the dangers faced within it, including Apophis.
